title: Implement ranking and response assembly for broad-object recommendations
description:
Source: RFC-008

Build the core recommendation logic after sphere resolution. This task owns candidate ranking, payload assembly, and the answer-oriented material returned to the agent.

Scope:
- rank lamp candidates inside the narrowed application subset using explicit business rules

- assemble compact recommended_lamps entries with url, image_url, preview, agent_summary, facts, and recommendation_reason
- include 1-2 portfolio examples tied to the resolved application sphere
- generate one bounded follow_up_question appropriate for the scenario

Acceptance:
- high-power scenarios do not return indoor low-power linear products when suitable outdoor/projector candidates exist
- the payload contains 2-3 useful recommendations, links, images, portfolio examples, and one clarifying question
- the output is compact enough for direct agent use without extra retrieval steps

title: legacy cleanup: migrate prompts tests and bench to canonical doc-search naming
description:
Goal
Remove legacy corp-wiki naming from agent-facing prompts, tests, and benchmark fixtures so the canonical naming is consistently `doc-search` / `doc_search`.

Why
The repo now treats `doc-search` as the canonical document retrieval path, but legacy names still appear in prompts, tests, and eval fixtures. This keeps the model exposed to two competing names for the same capability and makes later alias removal riskier.

Scope
- Update system prompt and skill-facing instructions to use only `doc-search` / `doc_search` except in explicit deprecation notes.
- Update unit/integration tests that assert or mention `corp-wiki-md-search` / `corp_wiki_search` when they are not specifically compatibility tests.
- Update bench fixtures, golden data, and routing assertions to prefer canonical naming.
- Keep a very small compatibility test surface only where backward-compat behavior is intentionally preserved.

Out of scope
- Removing the runtime alias implementation itself.
- Migrating legacy corpus files or storage paths.

Acceptance criteria
- Default prompts and routing docs no longer instruct or suggest the legacy alias as a normal path.
- Tests and bench assets use canonical names unless a test is explicitly labeled as backward-compat coverage.
- Repo-wide search shows legacy names only in clearly marked compatibility coverage, migration docs, or historical RFCs.
close_reason: Prompts, skills, tests, and bench fixtures now use canonical doc-search/doc_search naming; legacy alias references removed from active agent-facing surfaces.

title: routing index: replace heuristic doc routing with route cards
description:
Context
RFC-010 recommends replacing heuristic routing with a first-class routing index built from `corp_db` topics and document corpus topics.

Problem
Routing still depends on prompt wording, keyword heuristics, and guardrails more than on a clear retrieval catalog.

Scope
- Define route-card schemas for `corp_db_search` and `doc_search`.
- Publish document route cards from document manifests / sidecars.
- Add support for aggregated routing index loading in `core`.
- Make agent routing consult the routing index before choosing `corp_db_search` vs `doc_search`.
- Preserve explicit document/quote/fragment overrides.

Implementation notes
- The routing index should be explainable and inspectable on disk.
- Guardrails remain valuable but should sit on top of index-driven choices, not replace them.
- Start with lexical/tag-based matching; embeddings are out of scope.

Acceptance criteria
- `core` can load a routing index containing both DB and document topics.
- Representative company-fact queries route to `corp_db_search` via index lookup.
- Representative document-backed queries route to `doc_search` via index lookup.
- Routing no longer relies solely on hard-coded heuristics for these cases.
close_reason: Added route-card index generation, loading, and agent guardrails for corp_db/doc_search selection.

title: doc_search: decouple read path from writable /data/corp_docs state
description:
Context
`doc_search` currently assumes it can create directories and append usage stats under `/data/corp_docs` even for ordinary read-only search requests. In practice this makes retrieval depend on write permissions and on pre-created storage roots. The issue is visible in local verification where scripts using default `/data/corp_docs` fail with `PermissionError` on hosts without writable `/data`.

Problem
- `search_documents()` reaches `_discover_documents()`, which calls `ensure_document_layout()` and performs `mkdir` on every request.
- `tool_doc_search()` always calls `append_usage_stat()`, which also creates directories and writes JSONL even if the tool is only reading existing docs.
- This breaks read-only deployments, cold instances where storage is not initialized yet, and any environment where `/data/corp_docs` exists but is intentionally mounted read-only for the agent path.

Scope
- Split read-path discovery from write-path initialization.
- Make `doc_search` usable when live/cas/legacy roots already exist but write access is unavailable.
- Degrade usage-stats persistence gracefully when storage is read-only instead of failing the whole request.
- Introduce explicit initialization path for intake/maintenance scripts that need to create directories.

Implementation notes
- Retrieval should not call `ensure_document_layout()` unless strictly necessary for reading an optional path that already exists.
- Usage logging should be best-effort and non-fatal.
- Keep intake scripts free to initialize layout as before.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc_search` succeeds in a read-only environment with pre-existing corpus roots.
- Missing write permission for usage-stats path does not fail retrieval.
- Intake/maintenance scripts still create layout when needed.
- Add regression tests covering read-only retrieval and non-fatal stats write failure.
close_reason: Fixed

title: document intake security: tighten policy for legacy Office binary formats
description:
Context
The intake layer currently allows legacy binary Office formats (`.doc`, `.xls`, `.ppt`) but only applies macro/archive validation to ZIP-based formats (`.docx`, `.xlsx`, `.pptx`). This leaves an under-specified and weaker security posture exactly on the highest-risk file family.

Problem
- `.doc/.xls/.ppt` are in the allowlist with no equivalent validation for macros, encryption, or malformed binary content.
- The current code explicitly comments that these formats remain allowed and are expected to rely on later parsing paths.
- This conflicts with the RFC security model for hostile document intake and can create a risky ingest path for binary Office files.

Scope
- Revisit the allowlist policy for legacy Office binary formats.
- Choose and implement one explicit v1 policy: reject by default, or accept only behind an additional validation/conversion boundary.
- Document machine-readable rejection reasons for unsupported/risky binary Office files.
- Add tests for the chosen policy.

Implementation notes
- Simplicity and reliability matter more than broad format coverage in v1.
- If the safe path is not yet implemented, rejecting legacy binary Office files is preferable to silently accepting them.

Acceptance criteria
- Legacy Office binary handling is explicit, not accidental.
- Intake no longer accepts risky formats without equivalent controls.
- Rejection/acceptance behavior is documented in code and verified by tests.
close_reason: Fixed

title: RFC-007: security hardening for document intake and parsing
description:
Context
RFC-007 explicitly treats document processing as potentially hostile input handling. Even local-first LiteParse requires deployer-side limits, validation and isolation.

Scope
- Add bounded execution boundary for parsing/search orchestration.
- Enforce file size, page count, wall-clock, CPU, memory and concurrency limits.
- Restrict converters to an allowlist (`lit`, `rga`, `ugrep`, `rg`, optional LibreOffice/ImageMagick wrappers).
- Ensure upload path never writes directly to `live`; only ingestion can promote validated docs.
- Protect CAS root from agent write access.
- Define policy for encrypted docs, macros, malformed files and rejection records.

Implementation notes
- Do not introduce a parser microservice unless needed; v1 should stay simple.
- Security controls should apply both to intake and to parse-on-demand execution.
- Avoid raw user-controlled shell fragments in any filter command.

Acceptance criteria
- Document intake and parsing run with enforceable limits.
- Agent cannot write to document live/CAS roots.
- Invalid or risky files can be rejected with machine-readable reason.
- Security model is documented and testable.
close_reason: Added bounded parser execution limits, direct /data/corp_docs deny path, safe search_text execution, rejection records, and security regression tests.

title: RFC-007: observability and usage stats for doc_search
description:
Context
RFC-007 relies on usage statistics both for debugging retrieval quality and for deciding which documents should be promoted into `corp-db`. Without explicit metrics and traces the new search path will be hard to tune safely.

Scope
- Emit metrics for file_type, match_mode, cache_hit, duration_ms, parse_duration_ms and result_count.
- Add trace attributes / spans for backend selection and fallback path.
- Persist usage stats needed for promotion candidates: query, intent_class, document_id, selected_result_rank, answer_success when available.
- Keep backward-compatible routing observability where possible.

Implementation notes
- Stats should distinguish no-match from parser failure.
- Avoid excessive cardinality in metrics; detailed per-document data should go to logs/tables, not raw metrics labels.
- Make usage records reusable by later promotion jobs.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc_search` requests are observable by backend mode and latency.
- Usage stats are sufficient to build promotion candidate reports.
- Failures and no-match are distinguishable in telemetry.
- New observability does not regress existing bench/debug workflows.
close_reason: Added doc_search metrics, trace attributes, backend/cache telemetry, and persisted usage stats for promotion candidates.

title: Normalize unicode dash variants in bench contains checks

Update bench text normalization so contains_any/contains_all treat common dash variants as equivalent. Acceptance: the latest full bench run 20260403_013443Z_896b26 evaluates to 27/27 pass without modifying the correct answer.

The tests should verify that runtime answers are not built from benchmark compact previews and should fail if internal truncation ellipses leak into user-facing responses.

title: document normalization: parsed sidecars keyed by CAS hash
description:
Context
RFC-010 recommends shifting parsing from query-time to ingest-time and storing normalized searchable sidecars keyed by CAS hash and parser version.

Problem
Current `doc_search` still parses raw files on the query path. There is no canonical normalized sidecar layout produced during ingest.

Scope
- Introduce `/data/corp_docs/parsed/<sha>/...` sidecars.
- Generate at least `text.txt`, `pages.jsonl`, and `meta.json` during ingest/rebuild.
- Key parsed artifacts by content hash plus parser version / OCR config hash.
- Add rebuild commands for stale or missing sidecars.

Implementation notes
- `lit` is the primary normalization backend for multi-format documents.
- Text-first formats may still use cheap direct normalization if the output contract remains identical.
- Sidecar publication must be atomic enough that `core` never sees half-written parsed artifacts.

Acceptance criteria
- Ingested documents get normalized searchable sidecars.
- Parser version changes cause rebuildable invalidation.
- Parsed artifacts are addressable by content hash, not original filename.
- Missing or stale sidecars can be rebuilt via `doc-worker`.
close_reason: Added parsed sidecars keyed by CAS hash and version, normalization on ingest, and rebuild-parsed support.

title: RFC-007: intake lifecycle and CAS-backed document storage
description:
Context
RFC-007 требует простой и надёжный document intake path с дедупликацией. Документы не должны попадать сразу в searchable corpus, а физические дубли не должны размножаться по папкам.

Scope
- Ввести filesystem lifecycle: `quarantine -> cas -> live/rejected`.
- Реализовать content-addressed storage в `/data/corp_docs/cas/` на основе `sha256`.
- Ввести manifests/metadata records, которые ссылаются на CAS blob вместо хранения независимых копий.
- Ввести thin live-record / alias model для searchable corpus.
- Определить retention policy для rejected/quarantine objects и sweep policy для unreferenced blobs.

Implementation notes
- v1 dedup только по exact content hash; near-duplicate detection не нужен.
- Atomic rename/move only; new uploads must never write directly into `live`.
- Layout должен быть sharded (`ab/cd/<sha>`), чтобы не раздувать каталоги.
- Решение должно оставаться на обычной FS без object store и distributed storage.

Acceptance criteria
- Повторная загрузка идентичного файла не создаёт второй physical blob.
- `live` содержит только metadata/aliases или thin records поверх CAS.
- Ingestion может однозначно восстановить document record по hash.
- CAS design documented in code/docs and ready for parser/cache integration.
close_reason: Implemented CAS-backed intake lifecycle with quarantine/live/rejected/manifests roots, dedup by sha256, thin live records, and maintenance scripts.

title: legacy office policy: re-enable .doc/.xls/.ppt only after guaranteed lit path
description:
Context
RFC-010 recommends re-enabling legacy binary Office formats only after a guaranteed LiteParse normalization path exists.

Problem
Legacy `.doc/.xls/.ppt` are currently rejected for safety reasons. That is appropriate now, but the format policy must evolve once the new normalization stack is real.

Scope
- Add end-to-end tests for legacy binary Office formats through the guaranteed `lit + LibreOffice` path.
- Revisit allowlist/reject policy after that path is proven.
- Keep rejection reasons explicit for any still-unsupported cases.

Implementation notes
- This task depends on the worker and normalization stack being complete.
- Simplicity and safety win over broad format support if any backend remains unreliable.

Acceptance criteria
- Legacy Office support is either safely enabled via the new path or explicitly left disabled with documented reasons.
- Tests cover the chosen behavior.
- Public tool/skill descriptions match the actual runtime policy.
close_reason: Legacy doc/xls/ppt support is gated by doc-worker runtime with explicit tests and synchronized public descriptions.

title: RFC-006: compact corp-db company-fact payload

- Company-fact corp_db responses are materially smaller and still sufficient for short factual answers.
close_reason: Compacted company-fact corp_db payload in core tool formatter and covered it with tests

title: RFC-005: Fast path для portfolio examples по модели светильника
description:
Контекст
Нужно реализовать fast path для сценария "примеры реализации/объекты/портфолио для светильника <точная модель>" по RFC-005 (docs/rfc/RFC-005-lamp-portfolio-examples-fast-path.md). Сейчас данные в БД есть, но нет одного детерминированного server-side path lamp -> category -> sphere -> portfolio, из-за чего агент уходит в дорогую многошаговую orchestration.

Цель
Добавить новый kind в corp_db_search и довести сценарий до быстрого, стабильного и наблюдаемого end-to-end поведения без hybrid_search/wiki/shell на happy path.

Состав работ
1. Реализовать backend contract и handler в tools-api.
2. Добавить observability для нового kind и его фаз.
3. Обновить tool schema/propagation в core.
4. Добавить routing rule в системный prompt агента.
5. Закрыть unit/integration/manual smoke.

Критерии приемки
- В системе есть kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp с контрактом по RFC-005.
- Агент может ответить на запросы вида "примеры реализации объектов для светильника R500-9-30-6-650LZD" через один corp_db_search fast path.
- Для success path не используются hybrid_search, wiki и shell fallback.
- Путь диагностируем по logs/traces/metrics.
close_reason: Implemented the RFC-005 fast path end-to-end across tools-api, observability, tool definitions, agent routing, and regression coverage.

title: RFC-005: Реализовать kind portfolio_examples_by_lamp в tools-api
description:
Контекст
Нужно закрыть основную functional gap RFC-005: добавить один composed backend operation для цепочки lamp -> category -> sphere -> portfolio внутри существующего route /corp-db/search. Это ключевая задача, без нее весь fast path не существует.

Что сделать
- Добавить новый kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp в schema/validation CorpDbSearchRequest.
- Добавить dispatch branch в основном route tools-api/src/routes/corp_db.py.
- Реализовать handler _portfolio_examples_by_lamp(conn, req, limit, offset).
- Переиспользовать exact lamp resolve path от lamp_exact, чтобы получить canonical lamp row и не дублировать нестабильную логику match/serialize.
- Выполнить server-side chain: exact lamp resolve -> category lookup -> sphere lookup -> portfolio lookup.
- Возвращать compact response model по RFC: status, kind, query, filters, lamp, spheres, portfolio_examples.
- Корректно маппить empty cases: lamp_not_found, category_missing, spheres_not_found, portfolio_not_found.
- Не использовать hybrid_search, embeddings, shell или внешние источники.

Ограничения
- v1 без materialized view и без нового storage layer.
- Нужен компактный payload, чтобы не раздувать контекст агента.
- Поля lamp.preview/agent_summary/facts должны переиспользовать существующий serializer от lamp_exact.

Критерии приемки
- POST /corp-db/search принимает kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp.
- На success возвращается одна лампа, список spheres и плоский список portfolio_examples с привязкой к sphere.
- На empty path возвращается filters.reason без runtime error.
- На happy path весь сценарий выполняется в одном route без дополнительных retrieval обходов.
close_reason: Implemented portfolio_examples_by_lamp kind in tools-api with exact lamp resolve, sphere lookup, portfolio lookup, compact response contract, and empty-path handling.

title: RFC-005: Добавить observability для portfolio_examples_by_lamp
description:
Контекст
После появления нового RPC нужен отдельный наблюдаемый execution path. Цель этой задачи — сделать triage быстрым и однозначным по request_id: где именно цепочка остановилась и сколько заняли ее фазы.

Что сделать
- Добавить route-level metrics для kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp в существующую модель метрик tools-api.
- Добавить phase-level duration metrics для этапов lamp_exact, sphere_lookup, portfolio_lookup, response_build.
- Добавить child spans внутри api.request: corp_db.portfolio_examples.lamp_exact, corp_db.portfolio_examples.sphere_lookup, corp_db.portfolio_examples.portfolio_lookup.
- Логировать в structured logs поля request_id, kind, lamp_id, category_id, sphere_count, portfolio_count, status.
- Проверить, что success/empty/error paths различимы по логам и trace.

Почему зависит от backend
Сначала должен появиться сам handler и финальная execution shape, иначе observability будет навешана на нестабильный path.

Критерии приемки
- Новый kind различим в metrics и logs отдельно от прочих corp_db_search запросов.
- В trace видны child spans по ключевым фазам нового RPC.
- По одному request_id можно понять: лампа не нашлась, сферы не нашлись или портфолио пусто.
- Empty path не выглядит как transport/runtime error в signals.
close_reason: Added observability for portfolio_examples_by_lamp: phase spans, phase metrics, and structured logs with request_id/lamp/category/sphere/portfolio counts.

title: RFC-005: Обновить schema и transport для нового corp_db_search kind в core
description:
Контекст
После добавления нового kind в tools-api core должен знать о нем как о допустимом инструментальном пути. Это нужно, чтобы агент мог вызвать fast path через существующий corp_db_search client без ручных обходов.

Что сделать
- Обновить tool schema/enum и описание нового kind в core/tools/corp_db.py и связанных слоях, если это требуется текущей реализацией.
- Убедиться, что transport layer не требует специальных изменений и корректно пропускает новый kind в tools-api.
- Проверить сериализацию запроса и обработку ответа success/empty/error на стороне core tool wrapper.
- При наличии существующих тестов на corp_db tool обновить их под новый kind.

Почему зависит от backend
Сначала должен быть зафиксирован и реализован public contract нового kind в tools-api. После этого core можно синхронизировать с фактической схемой.

Критерии приемки
- Core умеет валидно вызывать kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp через corp_db_search.
- Новый kind описан в tool schema и доступен агенту.
- На стороне core нет рассинхрона между ожидаемым и фактическим contract response.
close_reason: Synced new corp_db_search kind through tool definitions and validated core transport preserves the specialized payload without contract drift.

title: RFC-005: Добавить agent routing rule для portfolio examples по точной модели
description:
Контекст
Даже после появления RPC агент не начнет использовать его стабильно без явного правила в system prompt. Текущий сценарий деградирует именно потому, что агент уходит в generic retrieval chain и fallback-поведение.

Что сделать
- Обновить core/src/agent/system.txt.
- Добавить rule для запросов про "примеры реализации", "объекты", "портфолио", "где применялся", "какие проекты были" при наличии точной модели светильника.
- Новое правило должно приоритизировать corp_db_search(kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp, name=<model>) раньше generic hybrid_search/retry/fallback веток.
- Зафиксировать поведение: при success нового RPC не вызывать руками sphere_categories, portfolio_by_sphere, hybrid_search, wiki или shell.
- Разрешить fallback только если RPC вернул empty и пользователь явно просит расширенный текстовый контекст.

Почему зависит от core schema
Prompt должен ссылаться на уже доступный и корректно описанный kind. Иначе агент получит инструкцию на несуществующий или невидимый инструментальный путь.

Критерии приемки
- Запрос вида "примеры реализации объектов для светильника R500-9-30-6-650LZD" маршрутизируется в новый kind как первичный шаг.
- При success path агент не уходит в hybrid_search/wiki/shell.
- Поведение fallback ограничено и предсказуемо.
close_reason: Updated agent routing and corp-pg-db skill guidance so exact-model portfolio/example questions go straight to portfolio_examples_by_lamp and avoid expensive fallback paths.

title: RFC-005: Покрыть fast path тестами и smoke-проверками
description:
Контекст
После реализации backend path, observability и routing нужен финальный verification layer. Без этого останется риск, что контракт есть, но агент все равно уходит в старую цепочку или что empty/error paths сломаны.

Что сделать
- Добавить unit tests для handler на success, lamp_not_found, category_missing/spheres_not_found, portfolio_not_found и runtime error mapping.
- Добавить integration test на POST /corp-db/search с kind=portfolio_examples_by_lamp и проверкой JSON contract.
- Проверить, что request_id проходит через logs/traces для нового kind.
- Выполнить manual smoke на сценариях: exact model with portfolio, exact model without portfolio, unknown model.
- Отдельно подтвердить, что запрос "примеры реализации объектов для светильника R500-9-30-6-650LZD" идет через один corp_db_search fast path без hybrid_search/wiki/shell на happy path.

Почему зависит от observability и routing
Финальная проверка должна подтверждать не только backend contract, но и реальное поведение системы end-to-end, включая трассировку и agent routing.

Критерии приемки
- Unit и integration tests покрывают success/empty/error paths нового kind.
- Manual smoke подтверждает ожидаемое поведение по типовым сценариям.
- Есть явное подтверждение, что happy path не использует старые дорогие fallback-механизмы.
close_reason: Added regression tests for the new fast path, validated current workspace with tools-api/core test runs, and covered the key success/empty observability paths.

title: legacy cleanup: final audit and removal gate for corp-wiki references
description:
Goal
Run a final repo-wide audit after migration tasks complete and remove the last non-historical corp-wiki references.

Why
Legacy names tend to survive in edge-case configs, tests, scripts, or docs. A dedicated cleanup gate reduces the chance of shipping a half-removed alias that later reappears in prompts or runtime.

Scope
- Perform repo-wide audit for `corp-wiki-md-search` and `corp_wiki_search`.
- Remove any remaining non-historical references in code, scripts, tests, docs, or bench assets.
- Confirm inventories and generated docs are consistent with canonical naming.
- Record any intentionally retained historical references and why they remain.

Dependencies
- Depends on the naming migration, legacy path migration, and alias removal tasks.

Acceptance criteria
- Repo-wide search for legacy corp-wiki names returns only intentionally retained historical references, if any.
- No active runtime, prompt, test, or operator flow depends on the legacy alias.
- Cleanup result is documented in issue closure notes or a short migration note.
close_reason: Repo-wide audit completed; remaining active code paths are clean of corp-wiki legacy names outside historical artifacts.

title: make core corp_db_search timeouts and transport errors diagnosable
description:
RFC-004 task 3. Harden core-side tool execution for corp_db_search so slow or failed calls do not surface as empty errors.

Deliverables:
- include exception class and timeout budget in ToolResult.error for corp_db_search failures
- split aiohttp timeout budget into connect/read/total settings
- reassess total timeout after backend fast path changes
- add focused tests for TimeoutError and transport failures
close_reason: Added diagnosable corp_db_search timeout and transport errors in core with split timeout budget and focused tests.

title: Make latency chart resilient to long case labels
description: Update the bench dashboard layout so the 'Latency by Case (ms)' chart no longer collapses when case_id labels are long. Move the latency chart into its own row beneath Outcome and Tags, increase the chart card height, and adjust ECharts grid/axis label behavior so Y-axis labels remain readable and X-axis labels do not steal excessive plotting space. Acceptance: latency chart has a dedicated wide layout, axis labels remain legible on current dataset, and dashboard still works on desktop and mobile widths.
close_reason: Completed

title: RFC-012 benchmark algorithmic validation
close_reason: RFC-012 implemented: artifact capture, algorithmic validation, comparison mode, direct_tool runner, dataset migration, and tests are complete.

close_reason: Implemented bounded artifact capture in core via run_meta, tool metadata, and /api/chat meta exposure.

title: Extend bench schema for execution and validation modes
close_reason: Extended bench dataset/result schema with execution/validation modes and artifact-aware result rows.

title: Implement algorithmic artifact evaluator
close_reason: Added algorithmic artifact evaluator with path resolver, selectors, and structured checks.

title: Add benchmark comparison and shadow reporting
close_reason: Added bench_compare.py for legacy vs algorithmic comparison and shadow reporting.

title: Add direct_tool mode to bench runner
close_reason: Implemented direct_tool mode in bench runner for deterministic corp_db benchmark cases.

title: Migrate benchmark dataset to algorithmic validation
close_reason: Migrated golden benchmark dataset to execution/validation modes with 38 algorithmic cases.

title: Add tests for artifact validation and comparison
close_reason: Added unit and comparison tests for artifact capture, evaluator, comparison, and direct_tool rows.

title: Add company-fact routing telemetry fields
description: Add low-cost observability fields for the company-fact fast path, including company_fact_intent_type, company_fact_fast_path, company_fact_payload_relevant, company_fact_rendered, and company_fact_fallback_reason. This task is limited to local metadata/log enrichment and does not implement the broader RFC-013 observability work.
close_reason: Added local company-fact telemetry fields in run meta and span attributes

title: Add benchmark and live agent cases for company facts
description: Extend benchmark coverage with explicit company-fact end-to-end cases such as 'Расскажи о компании' and 'Подскажи контакты компании.' Validate the live agent path rather than direct-tool only, so the benchmark catches false-negative final answers after successful corp_db payloads.
close_reason: Added live agent benchmark cases for company facts and validated focused benchmark subset

title: RFC-007: promotion pipeline from doc_search corpus to corp-db
description:
Context
RFC-007 keeps `doc_search` as long-tail retrieval and `corp_db_search` as hot-path retrieval. This only works if frequently used and stable documents can be promoted into `corp-db` through a deliberate pipeline.

Scope
- Define promotion candidate schema and manifest format.
- Build operator/report flow that selects candidates from usage stats.
- Implement ETL contract for turning promoted documents or chunks into corp-db-ingestible records.
- Define how promotion affects routing policy for short fact-like queries.
- Support re-promotion/re-ingest when source document hash changes.

Implementation notes
- Promotion is explicit and reviewable; no automatic silent promotion in v1.
- Keep chunking/normalization contracts simple and inspectable.
- Avoid tying promotion to one file path; source of truth should be CAS-backed document identity and manifest metadata.

Acceptance criteria
- Operator can generate promotion candidate list from real usage stats.
- Selected document can be transformed into corp-db-ready chunks/records.
- Updated source hash can trigger re-ingest path.
- Routing can prefer corp-db for promoted content without breaking fallback to doc_search.
close_reason: Implemented promotion candidate reports from usage stats and corp-db export manifests/JSONL for selected documents.

title: Implement deterministic company-fact fast path render and early stop
description: In core/agent.py, after the first relevant corp_db_search(kind=hybrid_search, profile=kb_search) for company facts, render the final answer deterministically in Python and terminate the run without extra LLM/tool iterations. Reuse existing renderer helpers where possible and keep scope limited to short company-fact questions.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic company-fact fast path render with early stop

title: Preserve benchmark algorithmic validation after runtime contract split
description: Verify and, if needed, adjust bench/bench_run.py, bench/bench_lib.py, and related evaluation helpers so algorithmic validation continues to consume metadata['bench_artifact'] or direct-tool payloads after runtime tool outputs become full-fidelity. The benchmark must not depend on compact runtime ToolResult.output for correctness or performance.
close_reason: Preserved algorithmic benchmark validation and added coverage that evaluation uses bench artifacts instead of runtime answer shape

title: Add runtime-vs-bench output policy helpers and guardrails
description: Introduce a small shared policy/helper layer for tool executors so benchmark artifact builders cannot accidentally become runtime payload formatters. The implementation should make the separation explicit in code review and easy to reuse by future tools. Include naming cleanup where current helper names encourage mixing compact runtime and artifact logic.
close_reason: Added shared tool_output_policy helpers and wired corp_db/doc_search to explicit runtime-vs-bench contracts

title: doc_search runtime: switch core to sidecar-based read path
description:
Context
RFC-010 treats `core` as a read-only retrieval layer over normalized sidecars instead of a parser runtime.

Problem
Current `doc_search` still reads and parses raw documents inside `core`, including optional `lit` fallback and format-specific extractors.

Scope
- Refactor `doc_search` to use normalized sidecars as the primary search substrate.
- Remove raw-file parsing from the normal hot path.
- Keep result shaping, scoring, snippets, usage stats, and guardrails in `core`.
- Define a degraded behavior when sidecars are missing or stale (`normalization_missing` or similar) rather than reparsing raw files in chat path.

Implementation notes
- Production-critical read path should use the minimal toolchain: `fd`, `rg`, `jq`.
- Read path must remain usable in read-only environments.
- Preserve backward-compatible output fields where reasonable.

Acceptance criteria
- A normal `doc_search` request does not parse raw binary documents in `core`.
- Retrieval works from normalized sidecars and manifests only.
- Missing sidecars do not trigger heavy reparsing on the chat path.
- Existing routing and observability continue to function.
close_reason: Switched core doc_search to normalized sidecar read path with degraded normalization_missing behavior.

title: Harden exact numeric equality filters in lamp_filters
description:
Validation after RFC-004 showed that direct hybrid_search requests using exact numeric equality style filters can still return false empty results, even when a realistic range-style request finds the correct lamp.

Observed case:
- beam_pattern=60° + dimensions_raw=774 x 428 x 406 мм + weight_kg_min=18.3 + weight_kg_max=18.3 returned empty
- the equivalent softer request with weight_kg_max=18.3 returned the expected R500-9-60 lamp

Required work:
- investigate exact numeric comparison behavior for lamp_filters and hybrid_search lamp filtering
- check normalization/rounding across numeric(10,3) catalog fields and request parsing
- make exact-match numeric filters no more fragile than bounded range filters for values such as weight_kg, dimensions, power_factor_min, and dimension ranges
- add regression tests for exact equality and mixed exact+text filters
- document any intended tolerance/normalization policy in code comments or skill examples if needed

Acceptance criteria:
- exact equality style numeric filters work for known catalog cases that currently reproduce false empty results
- tests cover at least weight_kg and one other numeric characteristic
- no recall regression for existing range-filter behavior
close_reason: Hardened decimal equality filters with range tolerance and added regression coverage

title: Tighten company-fact payload relevance and guardrail success conditions
description: Refine company-fact success detection so corp_db company-fact success is only marked when the payload is relevant to the requested fact type. Contacts require extracted contact fields or contact-specific headings; about-company requires company-profile headings; website/year queries require extracted values. Ensure guardrail does not block fallback on weak payloads.
close_reason: Tightened company-fact payload relevance checks and guardrail success conditions

title: doc intake: repo inbox and sync-repo workflow
description:
Context
RFC-010 defines a simple admin-only repo workflow: documents are added to `doc-corpus/inbox/` and ingested by `doc-worker sync-repo`.

Problem
Current intake exists only as ad hoc CLI over arbitrary paths. There is no canonical repo inbox, no sidecar metadata convention, and no reproducible operator flow.

Scope
- Add `doc-corpus/inbox/` and `doc-corpus/manifests/` conventions.
- Extend the document ingestion CLI with `sync-repo`.
- Support optional `<filename>.meta.json` sidecars.
- Produce a structured report for ingested, duplicate, and rejected files.
- Keep the workflow admin-only and repo-driven.

Implementation notes
- The repo inbox is the source of truth for initial operator uploads.
- Runtime artifacts must still land in `/data/corp_docs/...`, not remain in the repo inbox.
- Rejected items must preserve machine-readable reasons.

Acceptance criteria
- Admin can copy a file to `doc-corpus/inbox/` and run one documented command.
- The command ingests all eligible repo inbox files into runtime storage.
- Duplicate and rejected outcomes are reported explicitly.
- Optional sidecar metadata is merged into manifests.
close_reason: Added repo inbox workflow, sync-repo CLI, metadata sidecars, and explicit ingest outcome reports.

title: document corpus security: block search_files bypass and align guardrails
description:
Context
The new document corpus boundary blocks direct `/data/corp_docs` access for `read_file`, `list_directory`, and `search_text`, and routing guardrails treat document-path tool usage as a blocked wiki/doc path after successful corp-db company-fact answers. However `search_files` still does not enforce path safety at all.

Problem
- `tool_search_files()` does not call `is_path_safe()` and can be aimed at absolute glob patterns outside workspace.
- An agent can still enumerate `/data/corp_docs/**` or other managed roots through globbing.
- Guardrail detection in `core/agent.py` does not classify `search_files` as a document-path attempt, so the wiki/doc guardrail can still be bypassed.

Scope
- Add path-safety enforcement to `search_files`.
- Treat `/data/corp_docs` and legacy wiki roots as blocked managed corpus roots consistently across all generic file tools.
- Update routing guardrail detection to include `search_files`.
- Add tests proving both direct access and guardrail bypass are prevented.

Implementation notes
- Preserve existing workspace globbing behavior.
- Avoid reintroducing shell-based path expansion as a workaround.
- Keep error messages consistent with existing file-tool denial semantics.

Acceptance criteria
- `search_files` cannot enumerate `/data/corp_docs` or legacy document roots from agent sessions.
- Guardrail blocks `search_files` after successful company-fact `corp_db_search` unless document context was explicitly requested.
- Regression tests cover both generic file-tool denial and routing behavior.
close_reason: Fixed

title: RFC-006: add bench route assertions for company-fact cases
description:
Implement phase 2 bench protections from RFC-006.

Scope:
- Extend bench evaluation/reporting to assert routing, not only answer quality, for company-fact cases covered by corp_db.
- Mark run_command and wiki skill file access after successful corp_db_search as regressions for those cases.
- Surface route failures in reports so latency regressions are visible even when answer quality still passes.

Dependencies:
- Depends on runtime guardrail and routing tests.

Acceptance:
- Bench reports explicitly fail or flag company-fact cases that route to wiki after successful corp_db_search.
close_reason: Added bench route assertions and dataset routing expectations for company-fact cases

title: RFC-007: canonical doc-search naming and routing migration
description:
Context
RFC-007 вводит canonical naming `doc_search` / `doc-search`, оставляя `corp_wiki_search` и `corp-wiki-md-search` как deprecated aliases на переходный период. Сейчас prompt, skills, tests и tool registry всё ещё завязаны на старое wiki-oriented naming.

Scope
- Ввести canonical tool name `doc_search` в core/tools и tools-api.
- Ввести canonical skill `doc-search` с multiformat document semantics.
- Сохранить backward-compatible aliases `corp_wiki_search` и `corp-wiki-md-search`.
- Обновить system prompt, skill descriptions, tool descriptions и routing examples так, чтобы agent выбирал между `corp_db_search` и `doc_search`.
- Обновить observability field naming только там, где это не ломает совместимость существующих bench results и dashboards.

Implementation notes
- Миграция должна быть additive-first: сначала aliases и dual-registration, потом switch prompt/tests, потом deprecation notes.
- Не ломать existing users of `corp_wiki_search` during rollout.
- В descriptions убрать markdown-only и wiki-only framing.

Acceptance criteria
- Agent prompt и skills используют `doc_search` как canonical path.
- `corp_wiki_search` остаётся рабочим alias.
- Existing tests can be updated without breaking backward compatibility.
- Naming in code and docs больше не вводит в заблуждение, что search ограничен wiki corpus.
close_reason: Implemented canonical doc_search naming with backward-compatible corp_wiki aliases across prompt, skills, tool registries, and tests.

title: Improve company-profile KB aliases and ranking in DB
description: Apply a minimal DB-side ranking improvement for company profile chunks in corp_search_docs/knowledge_chunks. Add targeted aliases for rows such as 'О компании', 'Контактная информация', 'Реквизиты', and 'Социальные сети компании', and adjust import/bootstrap logic if needed so generic company-fact queries rank the right chunks higher without a broad search redesign.
close_reason: Added targeted company-profile KB aliases in db/search_docs.py and rebuilt corp_search_docs

title: extend corp_db latency metrics with larger buckets and phase breakdown
description:
RFC-004 task 4. Improve metrics so long-running chat and corp_db search requests stop clipping at 10s and latency can be attributed to individual search phases.

Deliverables:
- extend histogram buckets for http_server_duration_milliseconds and corp_db_search_duration_milliseconds to cover long-tail requests
- add phase metrics for embedding, lamp_filters, hybrid_primary, token_fallback, alias_fallback, and merge
- expose the new metrics through /metrics and verify they are scraped by VictoriaMetrics
- update tests or smoke checks for metric presence
close_reason: Extended HTTP and corp_db latency histograms, added corp_db phase metrics, and validated exposure through Victoria metrics and tests.

title: Restore non-lossy corp_db_search contract in core
description:
Review showed that core/tools/corp_db.py now rewrites successful corp_db_search responses into a short human-readable summary. This is unsafe for production because it truncates results and drops fields that the agent may need to answer correctly for non-lamp or multi-row flows.

Problem:
- _format_result_summary emits only the first 5 rows and only up to 6 facts per row.
- It ignores structured fields from kinds such as sku_by_code and category_mountings.
- This is a regression versus the previous pretty-printed JSON tool output and can cause silent answer-quality regressions.

Required work:
- restore a non-lossy tool output format for corp_db_search in core
- preserve full response payloads for all kinds, including sku_by_code, category_mountings, sphere_categories, portfolio_by_sphere, and multi-match hybrid results
- if an agent-friendly summary is still desired, make it additive rather than replacing the underlying payload
- add focused tests proving that no fields/results are lost for representative kinds
- verify a sample response for lamp_filters, sku_by_code, and category_mountings end-to-end through core

Acceptance criteria:
- corp_db_search output available to the agent preserves all rows returned by tools-api up to the route limit
- no structured response fields are dropped for supported kinds
- tests fail on truncation/regression and pass after the fix
close_reason: Restored full corp_db_search payloads in core and added regression tests

title: promotion pipeline: consume normalized document exports in corp-db
description:
Context
RFC-010 keeps structured promotion separate from raw document normalization. `doc-worker` prepares normalized artifacts; `corp-db-worker` ingests promoted fragments into `corp-db`.

Problem
Promotion helpers exist, but they still derive chunks from the current mixed parsing path and are not yet aligned with a normalized-sidecar-first architecture.

Scope
- Update promotion export to consume normalized sidecars as the preferred source.
- Align promotion manifests with the new parsed artifact layout.
- Ensure `corp-db-worker` can consume exports without depending on raw document parsing.
- Preserve usage-driven candidate reporting.

Implementation notes
- Promotion remains operator-driven.
- Do not make `doc-worker` write directly into `corp-db`.
- Promotion should remain compatible with CAS-backed document identities.

Acceptance criteria
- Promotion exports are generated from normalized document content.
- `corp-db-worker` can ingest exported chunks without raw parser dependency.
- Promotion candidate reports remain available.
- Structured routing hints remain coherent after promotion.
close_reason: Promotion exports now consume normalized sidecars only and remain corp-db-worker compatible without raw parsing.

title: add trace spans and restore request_id triage through Victoria stack
description:
RFC-004 task 5. Make observability usable for end-to-end debugging of catalog retrieval latency and failures.

Deliverables:
- add child spans in tools-api for embedding, lamp_filters, hybrid_primary, token_fallback, and alias_fallback
- add a core span around tool.corp_db_search
- verify request_id correlation works in traces and logs for /api/chat and /corp-db/search
- close the current gap where VictoriaLogs or VictoriaTraces do not reliably support request_id-based triage
close_reason: Added child spans for corp_db phases and core tool span, then verified request_id correlation in VictoriaLogs and VictoriaTraces.

title: quality gates: tests, doctor, and observability for RFC-010 flows
description:
Context
RFC-010 changes both architecture and operator workflow. The project needs explicit regression gates and operator visibility to keep this reliable.

Problem
Without dedicated tests and observability, the migration to `doc-worker` and sidecar-based retrieval can silently drift from the intended design.

Scope
- Add regression tests for repo inbox ingestion, parsed sidecar generation, read-only retrieval from sidecars, routing-index selection, and promotion export.
- Add operator-facing status/doctor commands for `doc-worker` and parsed corpus health.
- Update docs/runbooks where needed.

Implementation notes
- Prioritize checks that enforce the new architectural boundary: heavy parsing stays out of the chat path.
- Tests should cover missing dependency and stale sidecar scenarios.

Acceptance criteria
- There are automated tests for the major RFC-010 flows.
- Operators have a documented way to inspect parsed corpus health and ingestion failures.
- Bench and regression tooling can detect drift back toward raw-file parsing in `core`.
close_reason: Added RFC-010 regression tests, doc-worker corpus doctor output, runbook, and parsed-sidecar bench signals.

title: RFC-007: parse cache keyed by CAS content hash
description:
Context
RFC-007 требует, чтобы expensive parsing and OCR were reusable across duplicate documents and repeated queries. Cache должен быть привязан к content hash, а не к пути файла.

Scope
- Ввести normalized sidecars (`text.txt`, `structured.json`, `meta.json`) for parsed documents.
- Key cache primarily by `sha256(file contents)` from CAS.
- Use file size / mtime only as fast invalidation hints before hash verification where relevant.
- Make cache reusable across multiple logical aliases pointing to one CAS blob.
- Add cache invalidation/versioning strategy based on parser version and OCR config hash.

Implementation notes
- Cache lives in `/data/corp_docs/cache/`.
- Avoid duplicating parsed artifacts for the same blob.
- Keep schema simple and inspectable.

Acceptance criteria
- Same CAS blob reuses one parse cache.
- Cache invalidates when parser version or OCR config changes.
- `doc_search` reports whether result came from cache.
- Repeated query on already parsed document avoids full re-parse.
close_reason: Implemented parse cache keyed by CAS sha256 with versioned cache directories and cache-hit reporting.

title: doc_search observability: preserve metrics/stats parity on corp_wiki_search alias
description:
Context
During migration the deprecated alias `corp_wiki_search` still remains callable. At the moment the alias reimplements a minimal wrapper over `search_documents()` and does not go through the canonical `tool_doc_search()` path.

Problem
- Alias requests do not emit the same metrics, spans, and usage-stat records as canonical `doc_search`.
- This creates blind spots exactly during the migration period when some traffic may still use the old name.
- Promotion analytics and debugging become inconsistent depending on which alias the agent selected.

Scope
- Make deprecated alias execution reuse the same observability and usage-stat path as canonical `doc_search`, or otherwise emit equivalent telemetry.
- Preserve alias-specific metadata only where needed for compatibility/debugging.
- Add tests covering alias parity for tool output metadata and usage logging.

Implementation notes
- Prefer a thin alias that delegates to the canonical implementation rather than a second partial implementation.
- Keep backward-compatible output fields where required, but do not fork the instrumentation path.

Acceptance criteria
- `corp_wiki_search` and `doc_search` produce equivalent metrics/usage records for equivalent inputs.
- Migration traffic is visible in promotion analytics and debug telemetry.
- Tests verify observability parity or intentional alias-specific differences.
close_reason: Fixed

title: Add tests for company-fact fast path and relevance checks
description: Add unit and integration coverage for company-fact subtype classification, canonical query rewrite, payload relevance validation, deterministic rendering, and guardrail behavior for contacts/about-company/company facts. Cover both positive and weak-payload cases.
close_reason: Added regression tests for subtype classification, rewrite, relevance, rendering, and route guardrails

title: Tolerate exact numeric equality filters in lamp_filters
description: Validation after RFC-004 showed that a direct hybrid_search request with weight_kg_min=18.3, weight_kg_max=18.3 and dimensions_raw for the R500-9-60 case returned empty, while the realistic agent-style request with weight_kg_max=18.3 returned the expected lamp. Investigate exact numeric equality handling and normalization in lamp_filters so exact-match numeric filters are not more fragile than range filters.

title: Fix corp_db rollout smoke and eval runbook for prod sign-off
description:
The current rollout runbook cannot produce a trustworthy sign-off because the RFC-004 smoke instructions generate a subset dataset in a tracked path and then evaluate results against the full golden dataset by default.

Problem:
- docs/operations/bench-runbook.md creates bench/golden/rfc004-smoke.jsonl inside the tracked golden directory
- the follow-up bench_eval invocation omits --dataset for the smoke subset
- this causes missing_result noise and a false fail during operator validation

Required work:
- update the rollout runbook so smoke subset generation uses a safe temporary or explicit path
- make the evaluation command explicitly reference the same subset dataset
- include a short operator checklist for interpreting upstream LLM 429 failures separately from search regressions
- ensure the documented workflow produces a valid pass/fail summary for the intended smoke scope
- optionally add a helper script if that materially reduces operator error

Acceptance criteria:
- the documented smoke/eval flow works as written on a subset dataset without false missing_result failures
- the runbook clearly distinguishes external LLM quota failures from corp_db/search regressions
- operators can use the documented commands to produce prod sign-off evidence
close_reason: Updated smoke runbook to use temp dataset, explicit --dataset eval, and LLM-429 triage guidance

title: legacy cleanup: remove corp_wiki_search tool and corp-wiki-md-search skill alias
description:
Goal
Delete the deprecated runtime alias layer once canonical naming is fully adopted.

Why
Keeping both `doc_search` and `corp_wiki_search` registered in runtime/tooling leaves two names for one capability, increases routing ambiguity for the model, and expands the surface area that must be tested and maintained.

Scope
- Remove deprecated alias tool registration from core and tools-api.
- Remove the `core/tools/corp_wiki.py` alias wrapper once callers are migrated.
- Remove the deprecated `shared_skills/skills/corp-wiki-md-search` wrapper once no runtime path depends on it.
- Update docs and inventories so only canonical tool/skill names remain.
- Preserve a clear changelog/migration note for operators if needed.

Dependencies
- Should start only after prompts/tests/bench and legacy storage paths are migrated away from the alias.

Out of scope
- Reworking doc-search retrieval behavior itself.
- Historical RFC cleanup beyond small forward references.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc_search` / `doc-search` are the only runtime-registered names for document retrieval.
- No active tool registry, skill registry, or API definition exposes `corp_wiki_search` or `corp-wiki-md-search`.
- Backward-compat references remain only in changelog/history where intentionally documented.
close_reason: Removed deprecated corp_wiki_search tool alias and corp-wiki-md-search skill/runtime files from core, tools-api, and tests.

title: RFC-004 corp_db search latency and observability hardening
description:
Implements RFC-004: speed up corp_db retrieval for filter-heavy catalog queries, remove empty timeout errors in core, and make Victoria metrics/traces/logs usable for end-to-end latency triage.

Scope:

- delayed embeddings and fallback pruning
- core-side timeout/error diagnostics

title: tools-api fast path for explicit lamp filters
description:
RFC-004 task 1. Rework hybrid_search so explicit lamp filters use authoritative lamp_filters as the first decision point instead of always paying for the full hybrid path.

Deliverables:
- call lamp_filters before expensive hybrid stages when explicit lamp filters are present
- return hybrid_search response immediately with a stable search_strategy when filter results are sufficient
- preserve current corp_db_search API contract and payload shape
- add tests proving token fallback is skipped when lamp_filters already returned valid results
close_reason: Implemented fast path for explicit lamp filters in hybrid_search with authoritative lamp_filters results and fallback skip tests.

title: RFC-007: doc_search engine v2 with Unix-style execution graph
description:
Context
`doc_search` должен стать bounded multiformat retrieval tool. RFC-007 специально уходит от одного жёсткого pipeline и предлагает allowlisted execution graph вокруг `fd`, `rg`, `rga`, `ugrep`, `liteparse`, `jq`.

Scope
- Реализовать canonical `doc_search` tool executor.
- Добавить multi-root discovery (`legacy wiki root`, `document live root`).
- Добавить file type classification и backend selection policy.
- Поддержать allowlisted execution patterns, включая text-first search и file-scoped `liteparse` execution.
- Вернуть нормализованный structured result contract: document_id, relative_path, file_type, match_mode, snippet, page/sheet/slide, cache_hit, score.

Implementation notes
- Avoid agent-level shell composition; orchestration lives inside bounded executor.
- `liteparse` не должен быть universal first step.
- Prefer cheap adapters (`rg`/`rga`/`ugrep`) before parse-on-demand.
- Support stdin/stdout-friendly patterns where that simplifies reliable execution.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc_search` can search at least md/pdf/docx/xlsx/pptx/png/jpg/tiff according to RFC-007.
- Tool returns structured payload instead of ad hoc markdown dump.
- Execution graph is allowlisted and observable.
- Existing alias `corp_wiki_search` can delegate to the new engine.
close_reason: Implemented doc_search engine v2 with multi-root discovery, multiformat extraction, legacy wiki compatibility, and structured results.

title: legacy cleanup: migrate off corp-wiki legacy corpus path
description:
Goal
Remove direct dependence on the legacy wiki storage path and make canonical doc-corpus ingestion the only supported source for searchable documents.

Why
`doc_search` still knows about the old `/data/skills/corp-wiki-md-search/wiki` location. That keeps a hidden second source of truth for documents, complicates operator workflows, and blocks complete removal of the legacy skill.

Scope
- Inventory any remaining reads from `/data/skills/corp-wiki-md-search/wiki` or equivalent legacy paths.
- Move remaining seed/example documents into the canonical doc-corpus / doc-worker intake flow.
- Remove fallback discovery logic tied to the legacy wiki directory.
- Update operator docs/runbooks so document availability is explained only through canonical inbox -> worker -> live flow.
- Add or update migration notes for existing deployments that still have documents in the legacy path.

Out of scope
- Changing CAS/sidecar formats.
- Removing prompt/test references unless directly required for migration docs.

Acceptance criteria
- `doc_search` no longer reads from the legacy corp-wiki filesystem path.
- All supported searchable docs come from canonical manifests/sidecars under the doc-worker managed corpus.
- Operators have a documented migration path for legacy markdown/wiki content.
close_reason: doc_search now reads only managed corp_docs manifests/sidecars; seed docs moved into doc-corpus/inbox and operator migration docs updated.

RFC-004 task 6. Finish rollout by validating that the new search strategy and observability signals improve real requests and are documented for operators.

Deliverables:

Source: docs/rfc/RFC-008-application-driven-lighting-recommendation-fast-path.md

Implement the RFC-008 broad-object recommendation path for catalog questions such as stadium, quarry, airport, warehouse, office, and aggressive-environment scenarios.

title: doc-worker foundation: add operator-side LiteParse service
description:
Context
RFC-010 recommends integrating LiteParse through a dedicated operator-side worker instead of putting heavy parsing dependencies into the `core` hot path.

Problem
Today the project has no dedicated document worker service. `core` remains the only plausible place for parsing, which conflicts with the platform architecture and makes future `lit` integration heavier and less safe.

Scope
- Add a new operator-side `doc-worker` service and Docker image.
- Install the required runtime stack into that image: `lit`, `LibreOffice`, `ImageMagick`, `fd`, `rg`, `jq`.
- Mount repo root read-only and `/data` writable.
- Define command entrypoints for `sync-repo`, `ingest`, `rebuild-parsed`, `rebuild-routes`, `doctor`.
- Add compose wiring under the `operator` profile.

Implementation notes
- Do not move chat-time retrieval into this service.
- Keep `doc-worker` as a write-path / maintenance component only.
- Resource limits should be higher than `core`, but bounded.

Acceptance criteria
- `docker compose` has a `doc-worker` service under `operator` profile.
- Running the container can execute placeholder commands successfully.
- `core` and `tools-api` do not gain mandatory `lit`/LibreOffice/ImageMagick dependencies.
- Service purpose and operator usage are documented in code or adjacent docs.
close_reason: Implemented doc-worker foundation with operator profile, Docker image, compose wiring, and CLI commands.

title: Route broad-object catalog questions to application_recommendation in prompt and skills
description:
Source: RFC-008

Update orchestration rules so supported object-based queries consistently use the new fast path instead of generic hybrid_search. This task owns prompt and skill behavior, not backend ranking.

Scope:
- update core/src/agent/system.txt with a special-case rule for broad-object recommendation questions
- update shared_skills/skills/corp-pg-db/SKILL.md to document the new kind and the expected answer contract
- require application_recommendation before generic hybrid_search for wording such as stadium, quarry, airport, warehouse, office, and aggressive-environment requests
- define terminal behavior after successful application_recommendation so the agent does not continue searching
- document that the first answer must include links/materials and one follow-up question

Acceptance:
- the agent routes supported broad-object prompts to application_recommendation
- after success the agent does not call hybrid_search, doc_search, or shell-style fallbacks for the same answer
- prompt and skill instructions are consistent with the backend contract
close_reason: Updated prompt, skill guidance, and core guardrails to route broad-object questions to application_recommendation

title: Add telemetry for runtime finalizer mode and payload format

This should support incident analysis without requiring benchmark-specific response formats. Keep benchmark artifact metrics separate from runtime payload-format signals.
close_reason: Added runtime telemetry for company-fact finalizer mode and payload formats in meta/span state

title: RFC-006: add routing regression tests for corp-db precedence
description:
Add automated coverage for RFC-006 routing behavior.

Scope:

title: Add application_recommendation kind to corp_db_search contract
description:
Source: RFC-008

Extend the corp_db_search public contract and backend dispatcher to support kind=application_recommendation. This task owns the API surface and route wiring, not the full ranking logic.

Acceptance:
- the new kind is callable end-to-end through corp_db_search
- invalid payloads fail with bounded validation errors
- successful stub responses can be serialized without extra tool changes in core

title: Implement application intent resolution and synonym normalization
description:
Source: RFC-008

Implement deterministic resolution from broad object wording to application sphere. This task owns normalization, synonym handling, typo tolerance, and ambiguity behavior.

title: doc_search resilience: isolate malformed document failures per file
description:
Context
The new `doc_search` engine performs local extraction across live and legacy documents. Today extraction helpers for ZIP-based Office files and parser-on-demand flows can raise exceptions, and the main search loop does not isolate those failures per document.

Problem
- A single malformed `docx`, `xlsx`, `pptx`, broken archive, parser timeout edge case, or unexpected extraction exception can abort the entire `doc_search` request.
- In a shared corporate corpus this makes retrieval brittle: one bad file can break unrelated queries.

Scope
- Add per-document exception isolation around extraction/parsing.
- Distinguish parser failure from ordinary no-match in the returned telemetry and logs.
- Preserve successful results from other documents in the same request.
- Add regression tests with malformed fixtures proving that search continues.

Implementation notes
- Prefer a narrow `try/except` around extraction of each document rather than swallowing unrelated control-flow errors globally.
- Emit enough telemetry to debug bad documents without crashing the whole search.
- Keep result contract backward-compatible where possible.

Acceptance criteria
- One malformed document does not fail the overall `doc_search` request.
- Search returns results from healthy documents in the same corpus.
- Parser/extraction failures are observable and distinguishable from empty search results.
- Regression tests cover malformed Office/XML and parser-failure scenarios.
close_reason: Fixed

title: Fix benchmark and routing regressions after algorithmic migration
description: Address RFC-012 benchmark failures by separating benchmark contract mismatches from real agent/tool regressions: map company entity alias for kb_search, improve document lookup routing, improve portfolio_by_sphere resolution fallback, and relax benchmark selectors where portfolio_by_sphere is sufficient.

title: Add leaf-category traversal and presentation metadata for application recommendations
description:
Source: RFC-008

Make the data layer capable of turning resolved spheres into presentation-ready category and portfolio material. This task owns hierarchy traversal and metadata enrichment needed by the new recommendation payload.

Scope:

- no agent-side manual parent/leaf traversal is required for the supported recommendation flow
close_reason: Implemented leaf-category traversal, metadata enrichment, and search-doc aliases for application recommendations

title: RFC-007: multi-format tests and bench regression gates
description:
Context
RFC-007 changes retrieval behavior, storage, parsing and routing. We need regression gates across correctness, latency and routing so the new document path does not silently regress like the old wiki path did.

Scope
- Add unit tests for file type detection, CAS path derivation, dedup by content hash, backend selection and cache invalidation.
- Add integration tests for multi-format retrieval (`md`, `pdf`, `docx`, `xlsx`, `pptx`, `png`, `jpg`, `tiff`).
- Add tests proving identical uploads reuse the same CAS blob and parse cache.
- Extend bench dataset/eval with doc_search routing and latency assertions.
- Cover guardrail behavior: short company facts stay on corp-db unless explicit document context is requested.

Implementation notes
- Prefer small representative fixtures over large corpora.
- Separate answer-quality failures from routing/security/parser failures in bench output.
- Historical runs should remain analyzable even if canonical naming changes.

Acceptance criteria
- CI catches regressions in dedup, retrieval routing and parser behavior.
- Bench covers new multi-format document scenarios.
- Routing assertions distinguish corp-db hot path from doc_search fallback path.
- Duplicate-upload and cache-sharing behavior is verified automatically.
close_reason: Added multi-format storage/search/security tests, bench routing alias handling, and updated golden routing assertions for doc_search.

title: Add golden-set and benchmark cases from docs/questions.md for application search
description:
Source: RFC-008 and docs/questions.md

Extend the benchmark dataset with application-intent cases derived from the internal question list. This task owns the benchmark track and intent separation rules.

Scope:

Acceptance:

- application cases validate intent separation and catch regressions such as low-power indoor results for stadium-style prompts

title: corp-db: proxy /v1/embeddings 404 отключает semantic branch hybrid search
description: По логам proxy за последние 20 минут tools-api регулярно получает 404 на POST /v1/embeddings. Из-за этого _get_query_embedding() в tools-api возвращает None, semantic_weight сбрасывается в 0, и hybrid search деградирует до FTS/fuzzy, что даёт empty на запросах вроде 'нефтегаз проекты портфолио' и 'прожектор 100 ватт'. Нужно либо добавить embeddings route в proxy, либо переключить corp-db на доступный embeddings backend.
close_reason: Fixed embeddings path by adding proxy /v1/embeddings local fallback, rebuilding corp search docs with embeddings available, and restoring semantic branch for corp-db search.

title: proxy: startup without secrets leaves /v1/* in 'LLM not configured' state until restart
description: Observed on 2026-03-27 during runtime verification. External egress works (api.openai.com reachable, status.openai.com 200), but local proxy returns 500 {"error":"LLM not configured"} for /v1/models, /v1/chat/completions and /v1/embeddings. Current proxy container has secret files mounted now, but startup logs show base_url/api_key/model_name were not found at process init, so LLM_BASE_URL stayed unset for the lifetime of the process. Need fail-fast or reload behavior when secrets are missing at startup, and startup/health signals that distinguish healthy HTTP listener from usable LLM proxy.
close_reason: Fixed proxy secret loading and readiness: proxy now runs with readable secret UID, reloads secrets at request time, exposes /ready, and reports usable LLM state separately from liveness.

title: observability: добавить traces и runtime metrics для corp-db rollout

Нужно спроектировать и добавить минимально достаточный observability контур для runtime и поиска по corp-db: метрики запросов/latency/error-rate, traces для tools-api/core/corp-db search path, и удобный способ проверки в проде.
